rationale: The citing paper uses Hong and Sternthal (2010) as more than a passing citation: it is invoked in the theory section as direct prior evidence that matching information presentation to consumer characteristics can raise processing fluency and improve judgments. The paper also references it when showing its own effects remain after controlling for domain knowledge, indicating the cited work informs model specification and interpretation. While not the sole foundation, the citation meaningfully supports the paper's fit-and-fluency framing.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the fit-based persuasion logic in Hong and Sternthal (2010) from prior-knowledge x processing-strategy matching to culture-based processing-style x interface matching in mass customization. It adopts the cited work as precedent for a fluency mechanism and as motivation to account for domain knowledge in its tests.
